info.title: PropProfessor MCP API
info.description:
Lean, fast odds analysis engine for AI agents. Screens 36+ sportsbooks across NBA, MLB, NHL, NFL, WNBA, UFC, Tennis, Soccer — ranks plays by sharp movement, consensus edge, and steam detection.

See the README for setup instructions and tool guides by user type.

servers.description: Local MCP server (stdio transport — use an MCP client to connect)

post.summary: Query multiple active leagues at once and return a consolidated ranked list. Best for daily discovery: one call instead of 5-6 separate league screens. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

includeAll.description: Include rows even when consensus or movement data is missing

leagues.description: Specific leagues to query. Default: [NBA, MLB, NFL, NHL, WNBA, NCAAB, NCAAF, Soccer, Tennis, UFC] — every league the PropProfessor backend supports.

lookbackHours.description: Odds-history lookback window in hours, default 6

market.description: Market filter applied across all leagues, default Moneyline. Deprecated — prefer the canonical "markets" (plural array) param for multi-market scans. Still accepted for backward compatibility; the handler maps a single market to a one-element array internally.

markets.description: Market types to scan. Default: ["Moneyline", "Spread", "Total"]. Overrides `market` if provided.

compact.description: When true, strip verbose payloads (lineHistory, scoreBreakdown, full odds maps) and return only essential fields per match. Reduces response size by ~90%. Default false. Does NOT affect history hydration — movement data is always fetched.

fields.description: Optional list of field names to return per row. Overrides compact when both are set. Example: ["game","selection","odds","edge","tier","kai"]

skipHistory.description: When true, skip odds history hydration entirely. Use this to speed up screen calls when you only need current odds/edges and do not need movement data. Default false.

include.description: Optional list of top-level response sections to include. Values: "freshness", "warnings", "resultMeta", "league". Example: ["resultMeta"] to get only ok+result+resultMeta. Default: all sections.

post.summary: Parse a natural language betting query and route to the best tool to call. Pure parser — no data is fetched by this tool.

query.description: Natural language betting query, e.g. "best plays on Fliff today", "Tatum over 29.5 points", "what should I bet on Novig tonight". Full sentence or shorthand both fine.

post.summary: Clear the score timeline cache used for tier trajectory tracking. Resets all historical tier data. Use when starting a new session or after config changes.

Query the +EV endpoint and return candidate plays for enabled books. Secondary discovery — use /screen for primary selection. Returns 0 rows on quiet days. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

sportsbooks.description: Optional target books such as Fliff, NoVigApp, FanDuel, or DraftKings

leagues.description: Required league filter, e.g. NBA, MLB, NHL, Tennis, UFC, Soccer. Omitting this will cause a backend 400 error.

marketTypes.description: Optional market-type filters such as Main Lines or Player Props

periodTypes.description: Optional period-type filters such as Full Game or Single Period

minValue.description: Minimum EV/value threshold. Optional here because the frontend Positive EV screen may already enforce this.

validated.description: When true, runs sharp-movement and odds-history validation on candidates. Default false.

league.description: Ranking league override when validating a single-sport candidate set

market.description: Ranking market override when validating a single-market candidate set

books.description: Optional sharp-book override for validation and odds-history queries

limit.description: Max number of validated rows to return

includeAll.description: Include rows even when consensus or movement data is missing

maxAgeMs.description: Treat rows older than this many milliseconds as stale

lookbackHours.description: Odds-history lookback window in hours, default 6

debug.description: Include verbose movement debug payloads such as filtered line history and dropped-point reasons, default false

post.summary: Query the Fantasy Optimizer for DFS-style player picks across fantasy apps (PrizePicks, Underdog, etc.). Returns fantasy plays with projected values, odds, and risk metrics. Requires a paid PropProfessor subscription with Fantasy Optimizer access.

leagues.description: League filter, e.g., ["NBA", "MLB"]. Defaults to NBA, MLB, NFL, NHL, WNBA, NCAAB, NCAAF, Soccer, Tennis, UFC — every league the PropProfessor backend supports.

sportsbooks.description: Sportsbooks to compare (fallback for fantasy apps without odds).

hiddenBets.description: Array of hidden bet objects to exclude from results.

post.summary: Line shopping: show every book's odds sorted best to worst with spread from best price. Auto-resolves league-specific market names.

game.description: Game matchup or team name to match

market.description: Market type, e.g. Moneyline, Spread, Total

selection.description: Player or team selection to match

books.description: Optional book filter - show only these books

is_live.description: Whether to query live odds. Deprecated alias — prefer the canonical "live" param (same value, cleaner name). Both names accepted.

live.description: Whether to query live odds. Canonical name (preferred over "is_live").

post.summary: Check for new sharp line movements, steam moves, and significant odds changes since you last checked.

leagues.description: Leagues to check for alerts. Default: NBA, MLB, NFL, NHL, WNBA, NCAAB, NCAAF, Soccer, Tennis, UFC — every league the PropProfessor backend supports.

lookbackHours.description: How far back to scan for movement. Default: 6, min: 1, max: 48.

minSteamBooks.description: Minimum sharp books agreeing for a steam alert. Default: 2, max: 5.

post.summary: Returns markets available for a sport on a specific book. Use before quick_screen to know which markets to query.

book.description: Book name (e.g. NoVigApp, DraftKings). If omitted, returns default markets for the sport.

post.summary: View your logged betting history. Filter by status (pending/won/lost/push/all), league, recency, and limit. Returns most recent first.

status.description: Filter by result status. Default: all.

days.description: Only picks from the last N days

post.summary: Get your personal betting performance stats: win rate, profit/loss, breakdowns by league and confidence tier.

days.description: Only include picks from the last N days

post.summary: Get full details (line history, consensus, movement debug) for specific plays by game ID. Use after a screen call when compact=true hid the data you need.

league.description: League such as NBA, Tennis, MLB. Required.

game_ids.description: Game IDs to fetch full details for. These are the gameId values from screen rows. Deprecated alias — prefer the canonical "gameIds" param (same value, cleaner name). Both names accepted.

gameIds.description: Game IDs to fetch full details for. These are the gameId values from screen rows. Canonical name (preferred over "game_ids").

participants.description: Optional participant filter (team/player names). Mirrors the /screen "Participants" filter — only rows for these participants are returned.

live.description: Query live (in-play) odds instead of pre-match. Mirrors the /screen Pre-match/Live toggle. Default: false (pre-match).

lookbackHours.description: Odds-history lookback window in hours, default 6

post.summary: Get the recommended workflow based on use case (casual/intermediate/sharp). Call first to understand which tools to use.

user_type.description: casual: just wants top picks with plain English. intermediate: understands edge/tier, wants guidance. sharp: wants full movement data and control.

post.summary: Check auth freshness and endpoint connectivity. Reports token persistence state: persistedToDisk, refreshCount, and lastRefreshed.
Show current sport-specific ranking presets: sharp books per league, default market bundles, and preferred execution book.
Log a bet you placed before tip-off. Records game, league, market, selection, odds, stake, and optional metadata.

selection.description: Team or player you bet on

confidenceTier.description: Optional confidence tier from recommended_bets or quick_screen

kaiCall.description: Optional Kai call from the analysis (BET/CONSIDER/PASS)

post.summary: Manage bet visibility for the /fantasy table. action='list' returns all hidden bets, 'hide' requires bet, 'unhide' requires id, 'clear' removes all.

action.description: Operation to perform on the hidden bets list

bet.description: For action='hide': the bet object to hide (includes betId, matchId, market, selection).

id.description: For action='unhide': the hidden bet ID to restore.

post.summary: Get MLB game-level context: starting pitchers, venue/park factor, weather at first pitch, and lineup lock status. Returns riskFlag for weather/park effects.

gamePk.description: MLB gamePk (game ID) from a prior screen row, e.g. "824503".

post.summary: Validate a play and log it in ONE call. Runs validate_play, and if the verdict is BET/CONSIDER, logs the pick and returns a pickId for later settlement. If validate_play returns PASS, the bet is rejected up front with the reasons — nothing is logged.

gameId.description: Optional gameId from a prior screen call

selection.description: Team or player you want to bet

odds.description: Optional odds override; defaults to the validated play odds

post.summary: Get recent news, tweets, and a computed risk flag for a player. Use before placing a bet — if riskFlag is high, downgrade or skip the play.

gameTime.description: Optional ISO timestamp of the game

maxAgeMinutes.description: How far back to search, default 60

useXurl.description: Escalate to paid xurl CLI for real-time results. Bypasses cache. Use sparingly.

post.summary: One-call sharp-play scanner: target a book, scan all leagues × markets, get ranked results with player-context research and risk flags. Defaults to NoVigApp. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

books.description: Target execution books, e.g. ["Fliff", "NovigApp"]. Defaults to ["NoVigApp"]. Use a single book for focused results or multiple books for comparisons.

book.description: Single target book shortcut (alias for books: ["BookName"])

leagues.description: Leagues to scan, e.g. ["MLB", "NBA"]. Defaults to every league the PropProfessor backend supports: NBA, MLB, NFL, NHL, WNBA, NCAAB, NCAAF, Soccer, Tennis, UFC.

markets.description: Markets to scan, default ["Moneyline", "Spread", "Total"]. Overrides `market` if provided.

limit.description: Max bet candidates to return per league, default 10

scanLimit.description: Rows to scan per league/market before filtering, default 50

lookbackHours.description: Odds-history lookback window in hours, default 6

targetTiers.description: Tier filter, e.g. ["TIER 1"] to get only locks, or ["TIER 1", "TIER 2"] for recommendations. Defaults to no filter (return all tiers).

includeResearch.description: Run player_context research on each bet candidate. Default true.

debug.description: Include verbose movement debug payloads, default false

validateTop.description: When > 0, runs validate_play on the top N candidates per league/market before returning, merging verdictSummary and gameContext data into each row. Defaults to 0 (off) — the condensed scan already returns tiers, movement disposition, and research. Set to 3+ when you want deep validation with re-fetched consensus counts.

researchLimit.description: Max final plays to run player_context research on. Research is scoped to the returned (post-filter) plays, so this bounds payload size on large scans. Defaults to 50.

validate.description: When true (default), run validate_play on EVERY returned candidate before returning — merging validatedTier, validatedConsensusBookCount, validatedMovementDisposition, validatedRiskFlags, and validatedActionableSummary into each row. Eliminates the need for a separate validate_play call. Set to false for max-speed scans. When false, validateTop can still cap validation to the top N per league/market.

onlyBets.description: When true, return only candidates with finalVerdict === "BET" and finalConfidenceTier at or above minFinalTier. One-call "show me the bets". Requires validate (default on).

minFinalTier.description: Floor tier for the onlyBets gate. Default TIER 1.

kaiCall.description: Optional filter by display tier (BET | CONSIDER | PASS). Default: no filter. Example: ["BET"] for "bets only" or ["BET", "CONSIDER"] to drop PASS rows. Missing/garbage kaiCall is treated as PASS.

minEV.description: Optional minimum consensus edge percentage. Filters to +EV plays only (e.g. minEV=1.0 keeps plays with 1%+ edge). Default: no filter.

sortBy.description: Optional sort key. start = game time ascending (soonest first). edge = consensus edge descending (largest first). tier = TIER 1 first. consensusBookCount = most books first. riskScore = lowest risk first. Default: server-defined order (tier then edge). Missing-field rows always go to the end.

sortDir.description: Optional sort direction. Overrides the per-field default. asc = ascending, desc = descending.

topPick.description: When true, return only the single highest-conviction BET-tier play with a "why" rationale string. Use for one-call all-in betting.

lite.description: Token-light mode: returns only essential act fields (game, selection, odds, edge, clv, tier, kaiCall, startCST, movementDisposition, riskFlag). Avoids 200KB payloads. Implies compact.

cardWindow.description: Card window filter. 'today' returns today's slate plus any next-day matches merged in (flagged via nextDayMerged in the response). 'next' returns only tomorrow. 'all' returns every upcoming match with no date filtering. Default 'today'.

maxPlaysPerGame.description: Max plays shown per game in minimal verbosity (highest screenScore first). Default 2 to keep output scannable. Raise it (e.g. 10) when you want full coverage of a game without a second call. Standard verbosity always returns every candidate regardless of this value.

parseable.description: When true on minimal verbosity, the response includes a structured `plays` array (one object per candidate) alongside the summary string — so agents can parse without a second standard call. Default false (summary string only).

post.summary: Return only the highest-quality movement signals across requested leagues, ranked by signal strength. Each row includes tier, riskScore, kaiCall, and rationale.

leagues.description: League names to scan, e.g. ["NBA", "MLB"]. Defaults to every league the PropProfessor backend supports: NBA, MLB, NFL, NHL, WNBA, NCAAB, NCAAF, Soccer, Tennis, UFC.

limit.description: Max plays to return per league, default 10

market.description: Market type, default "Moneyline". Deprecated — prefer the canonical "markets" (plural array) param for multi-market scans. Still accepted for backward compatibility; the handler maps a single market to a one-element array internally.

markets.description: Market types to scan. Default: ["Moneyline", "Spread", "Total"]. Overrides `market` if provided.

targetTiers.description: Tier filter, e.g. ["TIER 1"] to get only locks, or ["TIER 1", "TIER 2"] for all recommendations. Defaults to ["TIER 1", "TIER 2"].

compact.description: When true, strip verbose payloads (lineHistory, scoreBreakdown, full odds maps) and return only essential fields per match. Reduces response size by ~90%. Default false. Does NOT affect history hydration — movement data is always fetched.

fields.description: Optional list of field names to return per row. Overrides compact when both are set. Example: ["game","selection","odds","edge","tier","kai"]

skipHistory.description: When true, skip odds history hydration entirely. Use this to speed up screen calls when you only need current odds/edges and do not need movement data. Default false.

include.description: Optional list of top-level response sections to include. Values: "freshness", "warnings", "resultMeta", "league". Example: ["resultMeta"] to get only ok+result+resultMeta. Default: all sections.

cardWindow.description: Card window filter. 'today' returns today's slate plus any next-day matches merged in (flagged via nextDayMerged in the response). 'next' returns only tomorrow. 'all' returns every upcoming match with no date filtering. Default 'today'.

maxPlaysPerGame.description: Max plays shown per game in minimal verbosity (highest screenScore first). Default 2 to keep output scannable. Raise it (e.g. 10) when you want full coverage of a game without a second call. Standard verbosity always returns every candidate regardless of this value.

parseable.description: When true on minimal verbosity, the response includes a structured `plays` array (one object per candidate) alongside the summary string — so agents can parse without a second standard call. Default false (summary string only).

validateTop.description: When > 0, runs validate_play on the top N plays per league before returning, merging verdictSummary and gameContext data into each row. Eliminates the need for a separate validate_play call.

validate.description: When true (default), run validate_play on EVERY returned play before returning — merging validatedTier, validatedConsensusBookCount, validatedMovementDisposition, validatedRiskFlags, and validatedActionableSummary into each row. Eliminates the need for a separate validate_play call. Set to false for max-speed scans. When false, validateTop can still cap validation to the top N per league.

kaiCall.description: Optional filter by display tier (BET | CONSIDER | PASS). Default: no filter. Example: ["BET"] for "bets only" or ["BET", "CONSIDER"] to drop PASS rows. Missing/garbage kaiCall is treated as PASS.

minEV.description: Optional minimum consensus edge percentage. Filters to +EV plays only (e.g. minEV=1.0 keeps plays with 1%+ edge). Default: no filter.

sortBy.description: Optional sort key. start = game time ascending (soonest first). edge = consensus edge descending (largest first). tier = TIER 1 first. consensusBookCount = most books first. riskScore = lowest risk first. Default: server-defined order (tier then edge). Missing-field rows always go to the end.

sortDir.description: Optional sort direction. Overrides the per-field default. asc = ascending, desc = descending.

topPick.description: When true, return only the single highest-conviction BET-tier play with a "why" rationale string. Use for one-call all-in betting.

lite.description: Token-light mode: returns only essential act fields (game, selection, odds, edge, clv, tier, kaiCall, startCST, movementDisposition, riskFlag). Avoids 200KB payloads. Implies compact.

post.summary: Mark a logged pick as won, lost, or push after the game ends. Updates your personal betting record for accurate stats.

id.description: The pick UUID from log_pick or get_pick_history

post.summary: Query /screen and return hydrated ranked rows with consensus, movement, and freshness metadata. Auto-resolves league-specific market names. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

market.description: Odds screen market, for example Moneyline or Player Points

games.description: Optional game ids or identifiers to filter the query

limit.description: Max number of ranked rows to return

historySportsbooks.description: Optional sportsbooks to fetch odds history for (overrides books for history enrichment only)

includeAll.description: Include rows even when consensus or movement data is missing

maxAgeMs.description: Treat rows older than this many milliseconds as stale

lookbackHours.description: Odds-history lookback window in hours, default 6

debug.description: Include verbose movement debug payloads such as filtered line history and dropped-point reasons, default false

compact.description: When true, strip verbose payloads (lineHistory, scoreBreakdown, full odds maps) and return only essential fields per match. Reduces response size by ~90%. Default false. Does NOT affect history hydration — movement data is always fetched.

fields.description: Optional list of field names to return per row. Overrides compact when both are set. Example: ["game","selection","odds","edge","tier","kai"]

skipHistory.description: When true, skip odds history hydration entirely. Use this to speed up screen calls when you only need current odds/edges and do not need movement data. Default false.

includeResearch.description: Run player_context research on each recommended play and attach riskFlag, riskSummary, and topTweet to the result. Use this to validate plays before placing a bet. Default true (research runs unless you pass includeResearch:false). Caches aggressively in player_context (30-min TTL, 5-min for high-risk).

riskDowngrade.description: When true AND includeResearch=true, plays with riskFlag="high" are removed from the recommendation. Without this, risk flags are just annotations and the plays stay. Default false.

playableOnly.description: When true, keep rows where the user-requested book is within the normal market range (executionQuality != "bad") even when consensusEdge is negative or zero. Use this when you want to find plays on a specific book (e.g. Fliff) at executable prices, not just positive-EV opportunities. Rows where the requested book is wildly off-market are still dropped. Default false.

include.description: Optional list of top-level response sections to include. Values: "freshness", "warnings", "resultMeta", "league". Example: ["resultMeta"] to get only ok+result+resultMeta. Default: all sections.

kaiCall.description: Optional filter by display tier (BET | CONSIDER | PASS). Default: no filter. Example: ["BET"] for "bets only" or ["BET", "CONSIDER"] to drop PASS rows. Missing/garbage kaiCall is treated as PASS.

minEV.description: Optional minimum consensus edge percentage. Filters to +EV plays only (e.g. minEV=1.0 keeps plays with 1%+ edge). Default: no filter.

sortBy.description: Optional sort key. start = game time ascending (soonest first). edge = consensus edge descending (largest first). tier = TIER 1 first. consensusBookCount = most books first. riskScore = lowest risk first. Default: server-defined order (tier then edge). Missing-field rows always go to the end.

sortDir.description: Optional sort direction. Overrides the per-field default. asc = ascending, desc = descending.

post.summary: On-demand sharp-play alert surface. Returns ONLY verified BET-tier plays (finalVerdict=BET, clean research) with new-vs-repeat dedup against a local store. No cron/polling. Call when you want "any new sharp plays?" — it will not re-alert the same play within the dedup window. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

minFinalTier.description: Floor tier for returned plays. Default TIER 1.

sinceMinutes.description: Only alert plays whose game starts within this many minutes (default 48h).

dedupWindowMinutes.description: Repeat window: same play alerted again only after this many minutes (default 6h).

post.summary: Analyze line history across multiple time windows (1h–48h) to detect sustained sharp-book consensus movement. Shows WHY a play ranks, not just WHAT ranks. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

league.description: League such as Tennis, NBA, MLB

limit.description: Max rows to fetch before analysis, default 100

lookbackHours.description: Total lookback for API query, default 48

minConsensusWindows.description: Minimum consensus windows required to include a play, default 0

sharpBooks.description: Sharp books for consensus, default [Pinnacle, BetOnline, BookMaker]

windows.description: Time windows in hours to segment history, default [1, 2, 6, 12, 24, 48]

post.summary: Scanner for the best target-book plays with supportive sharp movement across leagues and markets. Each row includes consensusStrength. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

targetBooks.description: Target execution books to scan together, such as ["Fliff", "NoVigApp"]. CANONICAL name for the books param — prefer this over all aliases below.

books.description: Alias for targetBooks. Use either books or targetBooks — both work.

targetBooksCsv.description: Comma-separated books for dashboard URLs (e.g. "Fliff,NoVigApp"). Deprecated alias — prefer the canonical "targetBooks" array. Still accepted.

book.description: Legacy single target execution book such as Fliff, NoVigApp, or Rebet. Deprecated alias — prefer "targetBooks" array. Still accepted.

targetBook.description: Legacy alias for book. Deprecated — prefer "targetBooks" array. Still accepted.

leagues.description: Leagues to scan. Defaults to every league the PropProfessor backend supports: NBA, MLB, NFL, NHL, WNBA, NCAAB, NCAAF, Soccer, Tennis, UFC.

markets.description: Markets to scan, default ["Moneyline", "Spread", "Total"]. Overrides `market` if provided.

sharpBooks.description: Optional non-target sharp comparison books override

limit.description: Max final sharp plays to return

scanLimit.description: Per league/market ranked rows to scan before final filtering

strict.description: Default true. When true returns only Bet candidates

includePasses.description: Include failed rows with passReasons for dashboard debugging

requireIndependentSharpMovement.description: Require non-target sharp support when true

requireBestPrice.description: Require target book to have the best available price

minMarketBookCount.description: Minimum market book count for prop classification

minSupportBookCount.description: Minimum support book count for prop classification

debug.description: Include verbose movement debug payloads, default false

kaiCall.description: Optional filter by display tier (BET | CONSIDER | PASS). Default: no filter. Example: ["BET"] for "bets only" or ["BET", "CONSIDER"] to drop PASS rows. Missing/garbage kaiCall is treated as PASS.

minEV.description: Optional minimum consensus edge percentage. Filters to +EV plays only (e.g. minEV=1.0 keeps plays with 1%+ edge). Default: no filter.

sortBy.description: Optional sort key. start = game time ascending (soonest first). edge = consensus edge descending (largest first). tier = TIER 1 first. consensusBookCount = most books first. riskScore = lowest risk first. Default: server-defined order (tier then edge). Missing-field rows always go to the end.

sortDir.description: Optional sort direction. Overrides the per-field default. asc = ascending, desc = descending.

post.summary: One-call bet evaluation: given a player/team and book, returns play details, validation verdict, best price across books, and staking recommendation. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

bankroll.description: Bankroll in dollars for staking recommendation. Default 1000.

post.summary: Mirror the website's "Smart Money" page: surface the $ volume (sharp action) and per-side odds range for each market — the sharp-money signal the +EV feed hides. Returns volumeUsd + oddsRange per game/market, sorted by biggest sharp action first.

leagues.description: Leagues to scan. Default: NBA, MLB, NHL, WNBA, NFL.

hideNCAAPlayerProps.description: Hide NCAA player props. Default: false.

post.summary: Given a bankroll, return stake allocations across recommended bets using fractional Kelly staking (TIER 1 = 2%, TIER 2 = 1% of bankroll). tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

leagues.description: League filter, e.g. ["NBA", "MLB"]. Defaults to all supported leagues.

market.description: Market type, default "Moneyline". Deprecated — prefer the canonical "markets" (plural array) param for multi-market scans. Still accepted for backward compatibility; the handler maps a single market to a one-element array internally.

markets.description: Market types to scan. Default: ["Moneyline", "Spread", "Total"]. Overrides `market` if provided.

compact.description: When true, strip verbose payloads (lineHistory, scoreBreakdown, full odds maps) and return only essential fields per match. Reduces response size by ~90%. Default false. Does NOT affect history hydration — movement data is always fetched.

fields.description: Optional list of field names to return per row. Overrides compact when both are set. Example: ["game","selection","odds","edge","tier","kai"]

skipHistory.description: When true, skip odds history hydration entirely. Use this to speed up screen calls when you only need current odds/edges and do not need movement data. Default false.

include.description: Optional list of top-level response sections to include. Values: "freshness", "warnings", "resultMeta", "league". Example: ["resultMeta"] to get only ok+result+resultMeta. Default: all sections.

post.summary: One-call daily briefing: the current sharp slate (top plays by consensus edge), your pending logged picks, and your recent betting stats (win rate / P&L). Replaces three separate calls (quick_screen + get_pick_history + get_pick_stats).

leagues.description: Leagues to include (default NBA, WNBA, MLB, NFL)

limit.description: Max plays to return from the slate (default 10)

post.summary: One-call bundle: screen + sort by game time + filter to BET/CONSIDER tier. Returns validated, executable plays for a specific book. Use when you want actionable bets for tonight without chaining multiple calls. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

leagues.description: Optional league filter, e.g. ["NBA", "MLB"]. Defaults to all leagues.

post.summary: Query a UFC card and return a shortlist with official plays, best looks, passes, and summary metadata. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

book.description: Primary execution book for the UFC card query

cardWindow.description: Card window filter such as today, next, or all

eventDate.description: Restrict the shortlist to a specific card event date

includePasses.description: Include pass rows in the shortlist response

limit.description: Max shortlist rows to return per bucket

markets.description: Optional UFC market bundle, with the first value used for ranked scanning

scanLimit.description: Per league/market ranked rows to scan before shortlisting

strict.description: Whether to return only bet candidates from the shortlist

upcomingOnly.description: Restrict results to upcoming UFC fights only

post.summary: Run all validation checks on a play in one call: re-fetch screen data, run player_context, check execution quality, return BET/CONSIDER/PASS verdict. tier/kaiCall/edge/screenScore are signal-quality ratings, not win-probability predictions.

league.description: League such as NBA, Tennis. Required.

gameId.description: Game ID from a prior screen_ranked row (gameId field). Required.

selection.description: Player or team name from a prior screen_ranked row (selection or participant field). Required UNLESS playId is provided.

playId.description: Canonical playId from a prior quick_screen / recommended_bets / screen_ranked row. When provided, row matching uses exact playId lookup and skips fragile string comparison — pass this to avoid "no row matched" lookup_failed errors. Takes priority over selection string matching.

market.description: Market type, default Moneyline. Required for correct row matching when playId is NOT supplied — use the league-specific market name (e.g. "Total Points", "Point Spread", "Draw No Bet", "Total Games").

books.description: Books to include in the validation, e.g. ["Fliff", "Pinnacle"]. Default uses the league preset.

book.description: Execution book — the book you intend to place the bet on. Used for the executable-price check. Defaults to the first book in `books` or the league preset preferred book.

lookbackHours.description: Odds-history lookback window in hours, default 6

skipResearch.description: When true, skip the player_context research step. Use this for ultra-fast validation when you only need odds/execution checks. Default false.
